factors.summary: The company is undergoing significant organizational changes, including workforce optimization projects and leadership adjustments.

evidence.text: We recently experienced senior leadership changes, and we continue to execute a number of significant business and organizational changes, including acquisitions, divestitures and workforce optimization projects to support our growth strategies. We expect these types of changes, which may include many staffing adjustments as well as employee departures, to continue for the foreseeable future.
evidence.subsection: Our ability to successfully manage ongoing organizational changes could impact our business results.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant organizational changes and workforce optimization indicate potential disruptions and cost management efforts.

factors.summary: The company faces regulatory changes that could increase costs and restrict operations.

evidence.text: Various statutes and rules regulate conduct in areas such as privacy and data protection which may affect our collection, use, storage and transfer of information both abroad and in the United States.
evidence.subsection: Data protection laws and self-regulatory codes may restrict our activities and increase our costs.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Regulatory changes may increase costs and restrict operations, posing challenges for the company.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving through efficiency initiatives.

evidence.text: Our restructuring and other productivity initiatives have been focused on a combination of improving operating leverage through targeted cost-reduction programs, business process improvements and portfolio restructuring actions, while at the same time investing in key programs to enhance future growth opportunities.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving productivity through efficiency initiatives suggests cost reduction and operational improvements.

factors.summary: Nielsen is successfully holding prices, contributing to revenue stability.

evidence.text: A significant portion of our revenue is generated from information (primarily retail measurement and consumer panel services) and measurement (primarily from television, radio, internet and mobile audiences) services. We generally recognize revenue from the sale of services as the services are performed and delivered to the consumer, which is usually ratably over the term of the contract(s).

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Stable pricing contributes to revenue but lacks clear indication of positive or negative trend.

factors.summary: Volume trends are stable with more than 70% of annual revenue committed under contracts, ensuring steady demand.

evidence.text: Typically, before the start of each year, more than 70% of our annual revenue has been committed under contracts in our combined Buy and Watch segments, which provides us with a high degree of stability to our revenue and allows us to effectively manage our profitability and cash flows.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: High committed revenue under contracts indicates stable demand and positive outlook.

factors.summary: End markets are growing through expansion in emerging markets and cross-platform services.

evidence.text: We continue to look for growth opportunities through global expansion, specifically within emerging markets, as well as through the cross-platform expansion of our analytical services and measurement services.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growth in emerging markets and cross-platform expansion offers significant opportunities.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable with a focus on efficiency and cost management.

evidence.text: Our restructuring and other productivity initiatives have been focused on a combination of improving operating leverage through targeted cost-reduction programs, business process improvements and portfolio restructuring actions, while at the same time investing in key programs to enhance future growth opportunities.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Efficiency initiatives aim to manage labor costs, but the evidence doesn't clearly indicate improvement or worsening.

factors.summary: Management maintains forward guidance with a focus on consistent revenue growth and expanding margins.

evidence.text: Our long-term financial objectives include consistent revenue growth and expanding operating margins. Accordingly, we are focused on geographic market and service offering expansion to drive revenue growth and improving operating efficiencies including effective resource utilization, information technology leverage and overhead cost management.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's focus on revenue growth and margin expansion signals a positive outlook.

factors.summary: Management is confident with their strategic direction, as indicated by investments in expansion and efficiency programs.

evidence.text: Our long-term financial objectives include consistent revenue growth and expanding operating margins. Accordingly, we are focused on geographic market and service offering expansion to drive revenue growth and improving operating efficiencies including effective resource utilization, information technology leverage and overhead cost management.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management expresses confidence in their strategic direction with clear objectives and expansion plans.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include managing high indebtedness and maintaining operational consistency across a global footprint.

evidence.text: Our high level of indebtedness requires active management of our debt profile, with a focus on underlying maturities, interest rate risk, liquidity and operating cash flows. Our operating footprint across more than 100 countries requires disciplined global and local resource management of internal and third party providers to ensure success.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: High indebtedness and global operational management pose significant near-term challenges.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy is clear, focusing on geographic and service expansion, supported by resource and technology investments.

evidence.text: Our long-term financial objectives include consistent revenue growth and expanding operating margins. Accordingly, we are focused on geographic market and service offering expansion to drive revenue growth and improving operating efficiencies including effective resource utilization, information technology leverage and overhead cost management.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear long-term strategy with a focus on expansion and efficiency improvements.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include maintaining global operational consistency, managing debt, and keeping up with technological demands.

evidence.text: Our growth objective of geographic market and service expansion requires us to maintain the consistency and integrity of our information and underlying processes on a global scale, and to invest effectively our capital in technology and infrastructure to keep pace with our clients’ demands and our competitors.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Sensitivities include managing debt and keeping up with technological demands, indicating potential vulnerabilities.

factors.summary: Catalysts include expansion in emerging markets and cross-platform service growth.

evidence.text: We continue to look for growth opportunities through global expansion, specifically within emerging markets, as well as through the cross-platform expansion of our analytical services and measurement services.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Expansion in emerging markets and cross-platform growth offer significant opportunities.

factors.summary: R&D investment is focused on enhancing future growth opportunities through targeted programs.

evidence.text: Our restructuring and other productivity initiatives have been focused on a combination of improving operating leverage through targeted cost-reduction programs, business process improvements and portfolio restructuring actions, while at the same time investing in key programs to enhance future growth opportunities.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Investment in targeted R&D programs aimed at enhancing future growth.

factors.summary: Adoption of digital technologies is enhancing efficiency through information and measurement services.

evidence.text: Our measurement and analytical services help our clients maintain and strengthen their market positions and identify opportunities for profitable growth. We have a presence in more than 100 countries, including many emerging markets, and hold leading market positions in many of our services and geographies.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Digital technologies are improving efficiency through enhanced services.

factors.summary: New products, such as Nielsen Audio, are successfully expanding service offerings.

evidence.text: On September 30, 2013, we completed the acquisition of Arbitron Inc., an international media and marketing research firm through the purchase of 100% of Arbitron’s outstanding common stock for a total cash purchase price of $1.3 billion. Arbitron has helped us better address client needs in unmeasured areas of media consumption, including streaming audio and out-of-home, and our global distribution footprint has helped expand Arbitron’s capabilities outside of the U.S. With Arbitron’s assets, we have expanded our Watch segment’s audience measurement across screens and forms of listening. Arbitron has been rebranded Nielsen Audio.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Successful expansion of service offerings with the acquisition of Arbitron (Nielsen Audio).

factors.summary: Supply chain health is stable with a global presence and focus on operational consistency.

evidence.text: Our operating footprint across more than 100 countries requires disciplined global and local resource management of internal and third party providers to ensure success.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates stable supply chain health with a global presence and operational consistency, which are positive attributes.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is managed efficiently through targeted cost-reduction and resource allocation programs.

evidence.text: Our restructuring and other productivity initiatives have been focused on a combination of improving operating leverage through targeted cost-reduction programs, business process improvements and portfolio restructuring actions, while at the same time investing in key programs to enhance future growth opportunities.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Efficient management through cost-reduction and resource allocation programs suggests effective utilization, a positive sentiment.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are managed effectively, supporting operational efficiency.

evidence.text: Our restructuring and other productivity initiatives have been focused on a combination of improving operating leverage through targeted cost-reduction programs, business process improvements and portfolio restructuring actions, while at the same time investing in key programs to enhance future growth opportunities.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Effective management supports operational efficiency, indicating a positive sentiment.

factors.summary: Product quality and safety are maintained, with no notable incidents or recalls.

evidence.text: Our measurement and analytical services help our clients maintain and strengthen their market positions and identify opportunities for profitable growth. We have a presence in more than 100 countries, including many emerging markets, and hold leading market positions in many of our services and geographies.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: No notable incidents or recalls, indicating strong quality and safety performance, a positive sentiment.

factors.summary: The company has implemented restructuring programs aimed at cost reduction through productivity initiatives, resulting in measurable savings. However, the restructuring charges have increased over time, indicating ongoing efforts to manage costs.

evidence.text: We recorded $51 million in restructuring charges for the year ended December 31, 2015, primarily related to employee severance associated with productivity initiatives.

We recorded $89 million in restructuring charges for the year ended December 31, 2014, primarily related to employee severance associated with productivity initiatives. We recorded $119 million in restructuring charges for the year ended December 31, 2013, primarily related to employee severance associated with productivity initiatives and contract termination costs.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Year Ended December 31, 2014 Compared to the Year Ended December 31, 2013

evidence.text: Our restructuring and other productivity initiatives have been focused on a combination of improving operating leverage through targeted cost-reduction programs, business process improvements and portfolio restructuring actions, while at the same time investing in key programs to enhance future growth opportunities.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While cost reduction initiatives have been implemented, the increase in restructuring charges introduces some uncertainty, leading to a neutral sentiment.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are unfavorably impacting the company's results, particularly due to changes in the Venezuelan exchange rate.

evidence.text: Fluctuations in the value of foreign currencies relative to the U.S. dollar impact our operating results. The average U.S. dollar to Euro exchange rate was $1.11 to €1.00 for the year ended December 31, 2015, compared to $1.33 to €1.00 in 2014.

We incurred $71 million in net foreign currency exchange losses for the year ended December 31, 2014, resulting primarily from changes to the Venezuelan exchange rate mechanisms as discussed in the 'Foreign Currency' section of 'Factors Affecting Nielsen’s Financial Results' as well as the fluctuation in U.S. to Euro exchange rate associated with our European revolving credit facility and the fluctuations in certain foreign currencies associated with intercompany transactions.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Year Ended December 31, 2014 Compared to the Year Ended December 31, 2013

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unfavorable foreign exchange impacts results, particularly from Venezuelan exchange rate changes, indicating a negative sentiment.

factors.summary: The company has been actively engaged in both acquisitions and divestitures, which have led to complexities in tax issues and strategic growth opportunities. However, there are significant risks associated with successfully integrating these acquisitions into existing operations, which could potentially impact the company's financial performance.

evidence.text: We have completed many material acquisitions and divestitures which have generated complex tax issues requiring management to use its judgment to make various tax determinations.

On September 30, 2013, we completed the acquisition of Arbitron... For the year ended December 31, 2015, we paid cash consideration of $246 million associated with both current period and previously executed acquisitions, net of cash acquired. Included in this amount is $45 million for an additional 13.5% interest in Nielsen Catalina Solutions...
In November 2015, we completed the sale of the National Research Group, Inc.... In June 2013, we completed the sale of our Expositions business...

We regularly evaluate opportunities for strategic growth through tuck-in acquisitions. Potential issues associated with these acquisitions could include... If an acquisition is not successfully completed or integrated into our existing operations, our business, financial condition and results of operations could be adversely impacted.
evidence.subsection: Failure to successfully complete or integrate acquisitions into our existing operations could have an adverse impact on our business, financial condition and results of operations.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Active M&A activity presents growth opportunities but carries integration risks, leading to a neutral sentiment.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are generally stable or improving across different segments, driven by revenue growth, cost management, and operational efficiency. However, there are slight variations in the margin trends depending on the specific segment or time period considered.

evidence.text: Gross and operating margins are stable with slight improvements.
Adjusted EBITDA increased 1.1% to $1,858 million from $1,837 million.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Year Ended December 31, 2014 Compared to the Year Ended December 31, 2013

evidence.text: Adjusted EBITDA increased 13.6% to $1,837 million for the year ended December 31, 2014 from $1,617 million for the year ended December 31, 2013, or 16.5% on a constant currency basis. Our Adjusted EBITDA margin increased to 29.21% for the year ended December 31, 2014 from 28.35% for the year ended December 31, 2013.
Non-GAAP business segment income increased 4.6% on a constant currency basis for Buy... Non-GAAP business segment income increased 24.0% on a constant currency basis for Watch.
evidence.subsection: Business Segment Results for the Year Ended December 31, 201 4 Compared to the Year Ended Dec ember 31, 201 3

evidence.text: Our long-term financial objectives include consistent revenue growth and expanding operating margins. Accordingly, we are focused on geographic market and service offering expansion to drive revenue growth and improving operating efficiencies including effective resource utilization, information technology leverage and overhead cost management.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvements in margins across segments, driven by revenue growth and cost management, indicate a positive sentiment.

factors.summary: Economic conditions, particularly in Venezuela and emerging markets, pose significant risks to the company. These conditions lead to foreign exchange losses, reduced consumer demand, and challenges in maintaining subscribers in regions with unstable economic environments.

evidence.text: Venezuela’s currency has been considered hyperinflationary since January 1, 2010. Changes in exchange rates resulted in foreign currency exchange transaction losses and charges of $8 million in 2015.

A number of adverse financial developments continue to impact the global financial markets. The current economic environment has witnessed continued malaise in consumer confidence and demand, impacting the demand for our customers' products and services.
evidence.subsection: Continued adverse market conditions, particularly in the consumer packaged goods, media, entertainment, telecommunicati ons or technology industries, could adversely impact our revenue.

evidence.text: In countries where there has not been a historical practice of using consumer packaged goods retail information or audience measurement information in the buying and selling of advertising time, it may be difficult for us to maintain subscribers.
evidence.subsection: Our international operations are exposed to risks which could impede growth in the future.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: The summary highlights significant risks from economic instability in Venezuela and emerging markets, including foreign exchange losses and reduced demand.

factors.summary: Demand trends are stable in the Buy segment and growing in the Watch segment, with increased demand for analytical services in developed markets driven by new clients and in emerging markets through investments in coverage and analytics.

evidence.text: Revenues within our Buy segment increased 3.4% (6.3% on a constant currency basis). Excluding the impact from the Harris Interactive acquisition, Buy segment revenues increased 0.8% (3.6% on a constant currency basis). Revenues within our Watch segment increased 20.4% (21.3% on a constant currency basis). Excluding the impact from the Arbitron acquisition, revenues within our Watch segment increased 4.9% (5.8% on a constant currency basis).
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Year Ended December 31, 2014 Compared to the Year Ended December 31, 2013

evidence.text: Excluding a 0.6% unfavorable impact of changes in foreign currency exchange rates, revenue grew as a result of increased demand for our analytical services as well as new clients in developed markets. Revenues from emerging markets increased... driven by our continued commitment to invest in coverage and analytics capabilities, which resulted in broad-based demand for our services within both our multinational and local client bases.
evidence.subsection: Business Segment Results for the Year Ended December 31, 201 4 Compared to the Year Ended Dec ember 31, 201 3

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is growing in the Watch segment and stable in the Buy segment, driven by new clients and investments in emerging markets.

factors.summary: Nielsen's organic revenue growth is mixed, with deceleration in the Buy segment and acceleration in the Watch segment. Overall, organic growth appears stable, though it is partially driven by consistent performance across segments and influenced by both organic growth and acquisitions.

evidence.text: Excluding the impact of the Arbitron and Harris Interactive acquisitions, revenues increased 2.4% (4.5% on a constant currency basis).
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Year Ended December 31, 2014 Compared to the Year Ended December 31, 2013

evidence.text: The increase in net cash provided by operating activities reflects stable organic growth and effective working capital management.
Excluding the impact from the Harris Interactive acquisition, Buy segment revenues increased 0.8% (3.6% on a constant currency basis).
evidence.subsection: Business Segment Results for the Year Ended December 31, 201 4 Compared to the Year Ended Dec ember 31, 201 3

evidence.text: Excluding the impact from the Arbitron acquisition, revenues within our Watch segment increased 4.9% (5.8% on a constant currency basis).

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Organic growth is mixed with some deceleration and acceleration across segments, but overall stability is noted.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks, particularly related to foreign currency exchange in Venezuela, are negatively impacting the company's financial results. The situation in Venezuela has led to significant challenges, including currency devaluations, hyperinflation, and foreign currency exchange losses.

evidence.text: We incurred $71 million in net foreign currency exchange losses for the year ended December 31, 2014, resulting primarily from changes to the Venezuelan exchange rate mechanisms as well as the fluctuation in U.S. to Euro exchange rate associated with our European revolving credit facility and the fluctuations in certain foreign currencies associated with intercompany transactions.
evidence.subsection: Consolidated Results for the Year Ended December 31, 2014 Compared to the Year Ended December 31, 2013

evidence.text: Our operations in Venezuela have faced significant challenges due to currency devaluations and hyperinflation, impacting our financial results.
evidence.subsection: Our international operations are exposed to risks which could impede growth in the future.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Geopolitical risks, especially from Venezuela's currency issues, are causing significant financial losses and challenges.

factors.summary: The company exhibits mixed signals regarding its debt leverage. While it has a strong balance sheet with a focus on deleveraging and maintaining financial stability, it also acknowledges significant indebtedness that could pose risks to its financial health. The liquidity position appears manageable, supported by strong cash flows and a revolving credit facility, but the substantial debt remains a vulnerability, especially in adverse economic conditions.

evidence.text: The company has a strong balance sheet with a focus on deleveraging and maintaining financial stability.
We believe we will have available resources to meet both our short-term and long-term liquidity requirements... combined with existing cash and amounts available under the revolving credit facility.
Our substantial indebtedness could have important consequences. For example, it could increase our vulnerability to general adverse economic and industry conditions.
evidence.subsection: Our substantial indebtedness could adversely affect our financial health.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: While the balance sheet is strong, significant debt poses a risk, particularly in adverse conditions.

factors.summary: The company is committed to returning value to shareholders through consistent dividend payments and potential share repurchases, reflecting confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: We remain committed to driving shareholder value as evidenced in 2013 with the adoption of a quarterly cash dividend policy by our board of directors, under which we have paid $408 million and $356 million in cash dividends during the years ended December 31, 2015 and 204, respectively.

We expect the cash flow from our operations... will provide sufficient liquidity to fund... dividend payments and capital spending over the next year... We may purchase, repay, redeem or retire any of our outstanding debt securities...

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Consistent dividend payments and potential share repurchases show commitment to returning value to shareholders.

factors.summary: The company's capital expenditure outlook is mixed. While the historical data shows relatively stable capital expenditures over the past three years, there is no explicit future guidance provided. The stable past investments suggest a cautious approach to growth, but the lack of forward-looking information in the liquidity section leaves some uncertainty about future capital expenditure plans.

evidence.text: Investments in property, plant, equipment, software and other assets totaled $408 million, $412 million and $374 million in 2015, 2014 and 2013, respectively.
We provide for additional liquidity through several sources including maintaining an adequate cash balance, access to global funding sources and a committed revolving credit facility.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Historical stability in capital expenditures but lack of future guidance creates uncertainty.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is stable and improving, supported by consistent operating performance and efficient working capital management. The increase in cash flow from 2014 to 2015 reflects a focus on working capital management and improved timing of customer and vendor payments.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities was $1,179 million for the year ended December 31, 2015, compared to $1,093 million for the year ended December 31, 2014. This increase was driven by the Adjusted EBITDA performance described above, and improved working capital performance on the timing of customer and vendor payments.

We have consistently generated strong cash flows from operations... providing a source of funds of $1,179 million... $1,093 million... $901 million... The increase of $86 million in 2015 is primarily due to our focus on working capital management...

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow is stable and improving, supported by strong operating performance and efficient working capital management.

factors.summary: Input costs are stable and manageable, reflecting the company's focus on operational efficiency and cost management. Despite the absence of significant fluctuations, the company has implemented initiatives to maintain control over costs and enhance future growth.

evidence.text: The company has not reported any significant changes in raw material or commodity costs that would impact financial performance.

Our restructuring and other productivity initiatives have been focused on a combination of improving operating leverage through targeted cost-reduction programs, business process improvements and portfolio restructuring actions, while at the same time investing in key programs to enhance future growth opportunities.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company maintains stable and manageable input costs with initiatives to control costs and enhance growth.

factors.summary: The company demonstrates a focus on talent retention with a stable work environment and competitive compensation, yet faces challenges in retaining key employees, particularly in growth markets with intense competition for skilled personnel.

evidence.text: The company has not reported any significant turnover or recruitment challenges in the provided text.

The inability to attract qualified employees in sufficient numbers to meet particular demands or the loss of a significant number of our employees could have an adverse effect on us, including our ability to obtain and successfully complete important client engagements and thus maintain or increase our revenues.
evidence.subsection: If we are unable to attract, retain and motivate employees, we may not be able to compete effectively and will not be able to expand our business.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faces challenges in retaining key employees, particularly in competitive growth markets.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting positively towards higher-margin segments such as Audience Measurement and Audio, particularly in emerging markets, which is driving growth and aligning with the company's long-term financial goals of consistent revenue growth and improved operating margins.

evidence.text: Revenues from developed markets increased... driven by increased demand for analytical services. Revenues from emerging markets increased... driven by investments in coverage and analytics capabilities. Audience Measurement (Video and Text) grew 5.3%, and Audio experienced significant growth.
evidence.subsection: Business Segment Results for the Year Ended December 31, 201 4 Compared to the Year Ended Dec ember 31, 201 3

evidence.text: Our long-term financial objectives include consistent revenue growth and expanding operating margins. Accordingly, we are focused on geographic market and service offering expansion to drive revenue growth and improving operating efficiencies including effective resource utilization, information technology leverage and overhead cost management.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix shifts positively towards higher-margin segments, driving growth and aligning with financial goals.

factors.summary: The company faces significant litigation exposure, including ongoing legal proceedings, potential intellectual property infringement claims, and antitrust investigations. These risks could adversely affect its operations, financials, and business practices.

evidence.text: We are subject to litigation and other claims in the ordinary course of business, some of which include claims for substantial sums.
Any such claims of intellectual property infringement, even those without merit, could result in our being required to pay possibly significant damages.
evidence.subsection: If third parties claim that we infringe upon their intellectual property rights, our operating profits could be adversely affected.

evidence.text: In the past, certain of our business practices have been investigated by government antitrust or competition agencies, and we have on several occasions been sued by private parties for alleged violations of the antitrust and competition laws of various jurisdictions.
evidence.subsection: Data protection laws and self-regulatory codes may restrict our activities and increase our costs.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant litigation exposure with ongoing legal proceedings and potential financial impacts.
